CREATE TABLE list_test_1 (id INT, vals LIST INTEGER);
SELECT id, vals <-> [1, 2, 5] AS distance FROM list_test_1;
CREATE TABLE list_test_2 (id INT, vals LIST INTEGER);
SELECT id, vals <-> [1, 3] AS distance FROM list_test_2;
CREATE TABLE t3 (id INT, a LIST INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE t4 (id INT, b LIST INTEGER);
SELECT t3.id, t4.id, t3.a <-> t4.b AS distance FROM t3 JOIN t4 ON t3.id = t4.id;
SELECT t3.id, t3.a <-> t4.b AS distance FROM t3, (SELECT b FROM t4 WHERE id = 1) AS t4;
CREATE TABLE complex_test (id INT, vals_a LIST INTEGER, vals_b LIST INTEGER);
SELECT id, vals_a <-> vals_b AS distance FROM complex_test WHERE vals_a IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT vals_a <-> vals_b FROM complex_test WHERE id = 2) AS nested_distance;
